method: 5 independent subagents launched in parallel, one per seam, no shared context

verdicts.key_finding: The SPA communicates with the backend exclusively via two HTTP endpoints via Angular $resource — zero server-side imports, shared state, or domain model references in the static files.
verdicts.reasoning: Coupling is minimal: all backend interaction is pure REST over $resource('albums') and $resource('appinfo'), making the boundary a clean HTTP seam. The data model is entirely free of persistence concerns. Test coverage is the dominant risk factor: no automated JS tests exist.

verdicts.key_finding: The Album domain object is saturated with JPA/Hibernate infrastructure — @Entity, @GenericGenerator, custom IdentifierGenerator, and a semantically ambiguous 'albumId' field alongside 'id' — meaning extraction must define a clean API contract from scratch.
verdicts.reasoning: Coupling is low-to-moderate: AlbumController depends only on a CrudRepository abstraction but the domain object drags Hibernate internals into the extraction boundary. Data model tangle is maximum: @Entity, @Column, @GeneratedValue, @GenericGenerator all present. Business criticality is maximum: Album CRUD is the entire user-facing function.

verdicts.key_finding: ApplicationInfo is a pure POJO with no persistence annotations, InfoController has no shared state beyond CfEnv and Spring Environment.
verdicts.reasoning: The controller is self-contained: reads CF environment metadata with no writes, no database access, no shared mutable state. Clean data model. No test evidence found. Business criticality is trivial — diagnostic/ops endpoints with no revenue or core user-facing function.

verdicts.key_finding: Runs before the Spring context is fully formed, directly mutates the live Environment (adding active profiles and injecting spring.autoconfigure.exclude), and has zero automated test coverage — a single mistake prevents any database backend from loading.
verdicts.reasoning: Coupling is maximal: hooks into pre-context ApplicationContextInitializer lifecycle, calls cfenv at startup, mutates active profiles, injects autoconfiguration exclusions. Zero tests. Business criticality maximum: a bug here causes the entire application to fail to start regardless of database backend.

verdicts.key_finding: Zero dependencies on any application module — imports only standard Java and SLF4J/Spring web annotations — trivially safe to delete outright.
verdicts.reasoning: Fully self-contained with no domain, repository, or service dependencies. Zero business value (pure chaos/test tooling). Actively dangerous in production (System.exit, OOM loop). Deletion is the correct action — not extraction.
